instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Online vs Offline Term Plan - Where to buy from?
Term insurance plans are available both online and offline. But which mode is the ideal one to invest in a term insurance plan? Let’s explore.
What is a term plan?
A term plan is a life insurance policy designed to protect against the risk of premature death. The plan covers deaths that occur during the policy tenure. If the insured dies within the tenure, the sum assured is paid as the death benefit.
Term plans have low premiums that allow you to opt for an optimal sum assured. This optimal sum assured provides your family with the financial assistance needed to meet their obligations. As such, with a term plan, you can buy financial security.
Comparison of online vs. offline term plan
Term insurance plans can be bought online, through the insurer’s website, or offline, through a life insurance agent or through the company’s branch office.
Here’s a comparative analysis of both the purchase processes –
What are the benefits of buying a term plan online?
If you buy a term plan online, you can stand to gain the following benefits –
Cost-Effective
As you can compare term insurance plans, you can compare the premium rates offered by different insurers. You can, then, choose the ideal deal that does not compromise on the coverage.
Moreover, buying online can eliminate the middleman and allow you to buy the policy directly from the insurance company. As a result, the commission payable to middlemen is cut down, and the premium reduces.
Thus, both these factors make term insurance plans all the more cost-effective and help in saving the premium outgo.
Claim Settlement Ratio
You can check the Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) of the insurance company online before you buy the policy. A company with the highest CSR is a better choice since it shows that the company settles its claims.
Zero Chances of Fraud
When you buy online, each detail of the policy is made available to you. You can check the terms and conditions and understand the scope of the policy. There’s zero chance of fraud or mis-selling since you get what you see.
Helps You Make the Right Choice
Insurance companies offer online term insurance calculators that help you find the ideal sum assured based on your income and other financial details. You can also compare and choose a policy that offers the idealcoverage matching your needs. Thus, buying online helps you make the right choice in choosing the term plan.
What should you buy: an online term plan or offline?
Both online and offline term insurance plans have their respective pros and cons. The choice ultimately rests on you, i.e., which is more suitable for you.
An online term insurance plan is a better alternative if –
You are tech-savvy and can buy the policy online after understanding the formalities of online purchases
If you have a working understanding of insurance and can understand the details of the policy
If you are self-sufficient to understand the claim process and handle claims
If you want to compare and do your research before buying the policy,
On the other hand, an offline term insurance policy will make sense if –
You trust your agent to offer you the ideal available coverage
You depend on your agent to renew the policy
You need your agent’s support at the time of claims
You are not tech-savvy
So, assess the suitability of both online and offline term insurance plans and then buy the right policy.
Things to Look for While Purchasing a Term Plan
When buying a term plan, whether online or offline, you need to be careful about a few factors and aspects of the coverage. These include the following –
The sum assured
The first thing to assess is the coverage level. Term plans are affordable, and so, you should ensure that you opt for an optimal sum assured for the complete financial protection of your family in your absence.
There are different methods available for calculating the ideal sum assured that you should opt for. Alternatively, you can use term insurance calculators that help you find the right coverage based on the details that you provide.
The coverage benefits
Next, you should check the coverage benefits offered by the policy. The benefits primarily include any inbuilt riders and the death benefit that the plan pays.
The available riders, inbuilt and optional
Check the policy for inbuilt or optional riders. Inbuilt riders provide additional coverage while optional ones can be added to make your policy more comprehensive. The riders come at a small additional premium but offer a good scope of coverage. So, choose suitable riders to make your term plan inclusive against possible contingencies.
Policy tenure
Term plans offer long-term coverage so that you can stay protected till older ages. Moreover, some term plans even allow you to opt for whole life protection. Check the tenure offered by the policy. Choose a plan that offers the maximum coverage, if possible, for your whole life so that you get complete security.
Premium paying tenure and frequency
Check the premium paying tenure available under the plan. Match it with your suitability to opt for a regular, limited or single premium payment.
Also, choose the premium paying frequency, i.e., annual, monthly, quarterly or half-yearly, based on your affordability.
Premium amount
Assess the premium being charged by the insurer vis-à-vis the coverage that is offered. Plans with the competitive premiums are better since they help you save on the premium costs and make the plan affordable.
Coverage exclusions
Term insurance plans have specific exclusions under which the promised death benefit is not paid. Check for these exclusions to know exactly what you are covered for and when your claim might get rejected.
Claim Settlement Ratio of the insurer
Lastly, check the Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) of the insurance company. Choose a company with a high ratio so that you don’t face any hassles at the time of claims.
Conclusion
A term insurance policy is a must-have for securing your finances and your family’s financial security in the event of any mishap. The plan is available for purchase online as well as offline. Each of the purchase modes has its benefits and drawbacks. Assess these pros and cons and then choose the suitable mode to invest in the plan.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Is there any benefit in comparing the online and offline purchase modes?
Yes, by comparing the online and the offline purchase modes, you can find out which mode is offering you the ideal term insurance policy. You can get a wider scope of coverage at comparatively competitive premium rates.
Is the offline mode of buying term insurance bad?
No, the offline mode is not bad. It is suitable for people who are not tech-savvy and who rely on their agents to help them continue the coverage with regular renewals and also at the time of claims.
Why are premiums costlier when buying offline compared to online?
There are two reasons why premiums are costlier offline. Firstly, you don’t get the facility to compare between the term insurance plans. As such, you get the deal which your agent offers or the company offer from which you buy the plan. Secondly, the middleman’s commission is included in the premium cost when you buy offline. This inflates the premium rate, and you end up paying a higher amount.
Do online, and offline term plans have different tax benefits?
No, the tax benefits remain the same whether you buy the term plan online or offline. However, if the offline premiums are higher, you can avail of a higher deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to the limit as specified therein.
Is it better to buy a plan without comparing it online vs offline?
No, a comparison of different term insurance plans is always important. It helps you find a policy that offers the comprehensive scope of coverage and that too at the ideal premium rates. So, always try and buy a policy after comparing it online.
What is the process of buying term insurance online?
To buy the term plan online, you have the following alternatives –
You can visit the website of the insurance company, choose the policy, upload your documents and pay the premium online.
You can download the mobile application of the insurance company and buy from the app.
You can visit an online marketplace that is tied up with different insurers, compare the available plans and then buy a suitable policy.
You can download the mobile application of online insurance aggregators and buy from the app.
What is the process of buying term insurance offline?
To buy the term plan offline, you have the following alternatives –
Contact your insurance agent, meet with him and buy the policy that he offers.
Visit an insurance broker and compare the different term plans that the broker has. Then choose to buy a suitable policy.
Visit the branch office of an insurance company from where you want to buy the policy. Apply for the policy offline, submit your documents and pay the premium in cash or cheque.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
input.compliance_comments: [Shweta Moorjani/Pune HO/Marketing/Life]: Tax approval (Context: No, the tax benefits remain the same whether you buy the term plan online or off...)
